response.justification: Maria brings 15+ years of polyglot fullstack engineering with a strong TypeScript/Node.js trajectory across multiple senior and staff roles, and her current position at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — a differentiating match for this role. Her testing culture is deep and genuine, evidenced by TDD practice across multiple engagements and authoring testing libraries in DLang. The main gaps are the absence of explicit NestJS experience (a primary framework) and only surface-level Kotlin/Java usage, but her demonstrated ability to pick up new tools quickly across radically different stacks makes these learnable risks worth taking.
strengths: Active AI-agent and MCP tooling development at re:cinq directly aligns with the role's AI agent development requirement
Consistent TypeScript/Node.js fullstack delivery across Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq with cloud (AWS CDK) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) exposure
Deep, authentic testing culture: TDD practitioner across multiple roles, created open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and ran community testing workshops
concerns: NestJS is a primary framework in the stack but is not mentioned anywhere in the resume — unclear whether she has hands-on experience or would need to ramp up
Kotlin is listed as a secondary requirement; Java appears only briefly (robotic arms at Altom) with no modern JVM backend work evident

Most architecturally significant backend work (Dunnhumby, high-throughput microservices) was done in DLang, not TypeScript — the depth of her Node.js/TS backend architecture experience is harder to assess
